mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in the federal funds rate amplify consumer demand and liquidity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-proxy baselines, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of co-occurring instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings growth, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during expansions, or 20-day forward returns show a negative correlation with INDPRO growth in > 60% of expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and higher aggregate wages, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in > 50% of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in housing starts and industrial production signal broad-based demand and supply chain utilization, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-occurring expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms robust economic expansion and supports corporate profit forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during stable growth phases.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns when GDP growth is between 1.5% and 2.5%, or forward returns show no significant difference from baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
